title: Systems and methods to measure or control fuel cell stack excess hydrogen flow using humidity
abstract: The present disclosure generally relates to systems and methods for managing and/or excess hydrogen flow in a system comprising a fuel cell or fuel cell stack based on humidity measurements.
claims: 17. A method of determining an excess fuel ratio of a fuel cell stack system comprising: flowing a first flow stream and a second recirculated flow stream, mixing the first flow stream and the second recirculated flow stream to form a third flow stream, flowing the third flow stream through an anode inlet manifold in a fuel cell stack, and comparing an excess fuel ratio of the fuel cell stack system to a target excess fuel ratio of the fuel cell stack system based on data regarding water content in the second recirculated flow stream via a controller, wherein the excess fuel ratio is equal to [(1−x_ H2O_RC −x_ N2_RC )m_ RC +m_ H2_P ]/[m_ H2_P ], and wherein x_ H2O_RC is a mass fraction of water in the second recirculated flow stream, x_ N2_RC is a mass fraction of nitrogen in the second recirculated flow stream, m_ RC is a mass flow rate of the second recirculated flow stream, and m_ H2_P is a mass flow rate of fuel in the first flow stream, and determining, via the controller, when to operate a blower based on the excess fuel ratio.
